equations.name: Arrhenius equation

primitives.comment: Ea/(RT): both Ea and RT are constants/products of constants — the ratio Ea/RT is a free terminal parameter combination

equations.superBEST_form: ELAd( A , EML( DEML( EXL( Ea/RT ) ) ) )  — A·exp(neg(Ea/RT))
equations.notes: Ea/RT is a ratio of free terminals, so the division itself is free. Only neg + exp + mul are counted. Standard Arrhenius kernel.

equations.name: Eyring (transition state theory) equation
equations.expression_latex: k = \frac{k_B T}{h} \cdot \exp\!\left(-\frac{\Delta G^\ddagger}{RT}\right), \quad \Delta G^\ddagger = \Delta H^\ddagger - T \Delta S^\ddagger

primitives.comment: ΔG‡ = ΔH‡ − T·ΔS‡ — sub costs 2 nodes via LEdiv/EML

ΔG‡/RT: ΔG‡ is computed above; RT is a free constant product; this is a general div = recip+mul = 2+2 = 4. However, the denominator RT is a free terminal, so recip(RT) costs 2 (EDL), then mul(ΔG‡, recip(RT)) costs 2 more.
kBT/h is a ratio of free constants — free terminal; mul costs 2 nodes

equations.superBEST_form: ELAd( (kB·T/h) , EML( DEML( EXL( EDL( RT ) , sub( ΔH‡ , ELAd( T , ΔS‡ ) ) ) ) ) )
equations.notes: kB·T/h is a product/ratio of free constants — free. RT is also a free constant product. The sub for ΔG‡ requires a full mul (T·ΔS‡) first. The div(ΔG‡/RT) cannot use the cheap EDL route because the numerator is not an exp node — must use recip(RT)·ΔG‡ = 4 nodes. neg and exp add 3 more. Final mul adds 2.

equations.name: Collision theory rate constant

primitives.comment: T^(1/2) — pow(x,n) costs 3 nodes via EXL
Ea/RT — ratio of free constants/parameters — free terminal

equations.superBEST_form: ELAd( A , ELAd( EXL( T, 1/2 ) , EML( DEML( EXL( Ea/RT ) ) ) ) )
equations.notes: This extends Arrhenius by adding the T^(1/2) prefactor. pow(T,1/2) is 3 nodes. Two mul operations chain A, T^(1/2), and exp(). The Arrhenius kernel itself is 5 nodes as in eq.1; pow adds 3 and an extra mul adds 2, for 10 total.

equations.name: Second-order rate law
equations.notes: Pure product of three free terminals (k, [A], [B]). Two chained multiplications, each costing 2 nodes. No exponentials, logs, or other complex operations.

equations.name: Integrated first-order rate law
equations.superBEST_form: ELAd( [A]0 , EML( DEML( EXL( ELAd( k , t ) ) ) ) )
equations.notes: k and t are both live variables (t changes), so k·t cannot be a free terminal and costs a full mul (2 nodes). Then neg (2) + exp (1) + final mul (2) = 7. Compare with Arrhenius where Ea/RT collapses to a free terminal because both are parameters/constants.

equations.name: Integrated second-order rate law

primitives.comment: 1/[A](t) on left side — recip costs 2 nodes via EDL

equations.superBEST_form: EDL( [A](t) )  =  EAL( EDL( [A]0 ) , ELAd( k , t ) )
equations.notes: The equation is expressed as an equality between two sides. Counting the full expression tree for the right-hand side computation: recip([A]0) + mul(k,t) + add = 2+2+3 = 7 nodes for RHS. The LHS recip([A](t)) adds 2 more nodes if we count the full mapping, giving 9 total. If the equation is used as a forward-evaluation of [A](t), we invert: [A](t) = 1/(1/[A]0 + kt), which costs recip([A]0) + mul(k,t) + add + recip(result) = 2+2+3+2 = 9 nodes identically.

summary_table.notes: Ea/RT is a free terminal ratio

summary_table.primitives: mul(T·ΔS‡), sub(ΔG‡), recip(RT), mul(ΔG‡·recip), neg, exp, mul(prefactor)
summary_table.notes: ΔG‡ computation adds sub+mul; div is general (no EDL shortcut)
Simplest — pure product of 3 free terminals
kt is live (not a free terminal); compare 5n for Arrhenius
add (3n) dominates; two recip nodes; no exponential

free_terminal_collapses: Ea/RT — both Ea and RT are parameters/constants, their ratio is a single free terminal
kBT/h — product/ratio of fundamental constants, free terminal
RT in Arrhenius denominator — free constant product
EDL_shortcut_applicable: recip([A](t)) — denominator of a single variable, 2 nodes
recip(RT) in Eyring when computing ΔG‡/RT
EDL_shortcut_NOT_applicable: div(ΔG‡, RT) in Eyring — numerator ΔG‡ is computed (not a raw exp node), so must use recip+mul = 4n
routing_notes.cost_drivers: sub for ΔG‡ requires a preceding mul (T·ΔS‡); general div when numerator is not a natural exp node
